Container.Name: Fodaris Agent
Container.Overview:
Fodaris Agent - lightweight monitoring agent that collects system metrics and sends them to your Fodaris server.
Collects CPU, memory, disk, network, temperature, GPU, process, and Docker container metrics. All data is encrypted in transit with AES-256-GCM.
Designed for monitoring remote Docker hosts, NAS devices, and other Linux machines. Install the agent on each machine you want to monitor.
Requires a running Fodaris server. Get your auth token from Settings > Endpoints in the Fodaris dashboard before installing.
Optional: Enable privileged mode in the container settings for SMART disk health monitoring and full hardware sensor access.
Container.Description:
Fodaris Agent collects system metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network, temperature, GPU, SMART disk health, processes, Docker containers) and sends them to your Fodaris monitoring server. Metrics are encrypted in transit with AES-256-GCM.
Use this to monitor additional Docker hosts, NAS devices, Raspberry Pis, or any Linux machine from your central Fodaris dashboard. Agents are also available for Windows and macOS at fodaris.co.uk.
Before installing, add this endpoint in your Fodaris dashboard (Settings > Endpoints) to get your auth token.

Config.Name: Server URL
Config.Description: URL of your Fodaris server. Use the server's IP address, not localhost.

Config.Name: Auth Token
Config.Description: Authentication token from your Fodaris dashboard (Settings > Endpoints).

Config.Name: Endpoint Name
Config.Description: Name shown in the Fodaris dashboard for this machine.

Config.Name: Verify SSL
Config.Description: Set to false if your Fodaris server uses a self-signed certificate (default). Set to true only for trusted CA-signed certs.

Config.Name: Collection Interval (seconds)
Config.Description: How often to collect and send metrics, in seconds. Default is 10.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Grants the agent read-only access to Docker for container monitoring. Remove if not needed.
